title: Avoid Calling Object.finalize() Method Directly
description: Directly calling the Object.finalize() method is misleading as it disrupts the contract that the finalize method should only be called once per object. This method is invoked by the garbage collector before it reclaims storage from an object that is no longer referenced. Hence, explicitly calling this method will perform operations that were supposed to be performed only when the object was not referenced anymore by any thread.
recommendation: Avoid directly invoking the finalize() method. Let the garbage collector handle this operation when the object is no longer referenced.
impact: Invoking the finalize() method explicitly can lead to unexpected behavior, since it runs code that was designed to only be executed at a different time. This may result in potential bugs and memory leaks.

checks.message: Avoid directly invoking the finalize() method. Let the garbage collector handle this operation when the object is no longer referenced.
